libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No external WebDAV server, Jackrabbit repository, Docker service, environment variable, or system property appears necessary. The project README describes jackrabbit-webdav as interfaces and common server/client utility classes rather than a deployable server; tests should use in-memory XML/protocol objects and simple javax.servlet request/response stubs or proxies.

deterministic_setup.reason: jackrabbit-webdav:2.0-beta4 exposes and implements servlet-based APIs such as DavServletRequest, WebdavRequestImpl, WebdavResponseImpl, InputContextImpl, OutputContextImpl, and header parsers, while its POM marks servlet-api as provided.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8851 [Automation] native-image run fails for org.apache.jackrabbit:jackrabbit-webdav:2.0-beta4; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Use javax.servlet APIs, not jakarta.servlet; this old Jackrabbit version predates Jakarta namespace migration.
If tests copy existing servlet stub classes that were written against servlet-api 2.4, they may need minor method adjustments when using the artifact-declared 2.3 API.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Meaningful WebDAV coverage commonly touches servlet-facing public APIs, but the artifact declares javax.servlet as a provided dependency, so the normal test scaffold may not include it transitively.
